justification: All 4 business_impact sub-fields (financial, operational, user, risk) are substantively populated with multi-sentence analysis. stakeholders_affected lists 7 distinct groups with specific impact per group (far exceeds the 3 minimum). 4 formal escalations for missing information are provided. Contractual SLA risk is explicitly identified in both the risk field and as a dedicated escalation. confidence.level is 45, well below the 80 threshold, with detailed assumptions listed. Competitive positioning is explicitly addressed in the risk field ('industry trend is toward MORE precision').

observations.text: Exceptional stakeholder granularity — distinguishes B2C vs B2B recipients, support team, operations, partner integrations, sales/account management, and product/analytics as separate groups with distinct impacts
Confidence level of 45 is well-calibrated given the missing contract information and unspecified business motivation
Contractual SLA risk is not only identified but escalated with concrete options (exclude B2B, review contracts first, or maintain internal commitments)
Competitive risk explicitly noted as counter-trend to industry movement toward more precise windows
4 formal escalations covering business motivation, service tier scoping, contract review, and API impact — each with options and recommendations
Financial analysis is balanced — identifies both positive (reduced SLA breaches) and negative (redelivery costs, churn risk) with estimated figures
Identifies API breaking change risk as a distinct concern, which goes beyond the rubric requirements
Operational impact is balanced — acknowledges short-term relief but flags long-term discipline erosion risk
